questions.question: What are the three defense layers for synthetic voice, and what does each handle?
options: Firewall, antivirus, and VPN

Anti-spoofing detection (is this clip synthetic? — handles non-cooperating adversaries), audio watermarking (imperceptible embedded signal — handles compliance for your own generated audio), and authenticated provenance (C2PA cryptographic signing + metadata)
Encryption, hashing, and salting
Volume limits, rate limits, and CAPTCHAs
questions.explanation: The lesson's rule: ship both detection and watermarking, or don't ship voice cloning. Detection is for content you didn't make; watermarking is for content you did.

questions.question: What changed in ASVspoof 5 versus earlier editions?
options: It only tests studio recordings

Crowdsourced (not studio-clean) data, ~2000 speakers (vs ~100), 32 attack algorithms spanning TTS + voice conversion + adversarial perturbation, and two tracks: standalone countermeasure and spoofing-robust ASV
It removed voice conversion attacks
It shrank to a toy dataset
questions.explanation: The realism jump shows in the numbers: SOTA is ~7.23% EER on ASVspoof 5 versus 0.42% on the older, cleaner 2019 LA set. Expect 5-10% EER on in-the-wild clips.

questions.question: What are the main detection model families?
options: Decision trees on MP3 headers

AASIST (graph-attention over spectral features, current SOTA on ASVspoof 5), RawNet2 (conv front-end on raw waveform + TDNN, simpler but competitive), and 2025 SSL-feature variants (ECAPA-style + WavLM + focal loss hitting 0.42% on 2019 LA)
Regex matching on transcripts
Human listening panels only
questions.explanation: The architecture trend mirrors speaker verification: self-supervised speech features (WavLM) layered under a discriminative head buy the biggest gains.

questions.question: What makes AudioSeal the 2024+ watermark default over WavMark?
options: It's the only one that's open source

Per-frame localized detection at sample resolution, jointly-trained generator + detector, robustness to MP3/AAC, EQ, ±10% speed-shift, and +10dB-SNR noise, a 485x-realtime detector (1000x faster than WavMark), and a 16-bit payload for model ID / timestamp / user ID
It embeds audible disclosure beeps
It works only on speech, not music
questions.explanation: WavMark's weaknesses — slow brute-force synchronization, removable by Gaussian noise or MP3 compression, not real-time friendly — are exactly what AudioSeal's design fixed.

questions.question: Why do you need BOTH detection and watermarking rather than either alone?
options: Regulators require redundant systems

Watermarks only mark audio from cooperating generators — an adversary's cloner won't embed one; detectors catch uncooperative fakes but have 5-10% real-world EER — together they cover compliance AND adversaries
They are the same technology
Detection only works on watermarked audio
questions.explanation: Complementary threat models: the watermark provides provenance for your own pipeline's output, while anti-spoofing classifies arbitrary clips where no cooperation can be assumed.
